comparisons.focusB: Practical Digital Skills (MS Office, AI, Typing)
comparisons.suitability: Coding builds logical structures, variables, loops, and math concepts. Digital Skills build immediate academic productivity, communication, formatting, typing, and digital hygiene. SkillNest recommends learning both in an integrated fashion.

AI prompting is highly accessible, using natural language to obtain creative or analytical outputs. Coding requires strict syntax and logical frameworks. AI helps build immediate design confidence, while coding builds computational logic. SkillNest teaches prompt engineering as a companion skill to Scratch block logic.

Online cohorts eliminate travel time, feature expert national instructors, and use interactive screen sharing. Offline centers often suffer from outdated hardware, lack of individual screens, and theory-heavy instruction.

SkillNest operates with interactive micro-batches (5-10 kids) and mandates practical projects (building a slide, creating a spreadsheet, designing a game). Traditional classes focus on memorizing textbook theory definitions for school exams.
Small cohorts encourage peer learning, collaboration, code reviews, and healthy competition. 1-on-1 tutoring can feel isolating and lacks the social dynamic of cooperative problem-solving.

Excel teaches tabular structures, cell variables, mathematical logic, and charts. AI tools teach unstructured content processing and creative synthesis. SkillNest combines both to prepare students for modern data productivity.
